content:
Four teams, four products, zero coordination — and the same four ingredients show up in every one.

12 min read
Just now

--

Read the article for free here.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

After a certain point in the chat, the agent forgets what you asked it to do. The context window fills with tool output, the plan disappears into the noise, and the agent starts solving the wrong problem.
Anthropic hit this wall building Claude Code. OpenAI hit it on Deep Research. A Chinese startup called Butterfly Effect rebuilt Manus four times before they got around it. None of these teams coordinated but all three landed on the same fix.
In July 2025, LangChain wrote it down: “a planning tool, sub agents, access to a file system, and a detailed prompt.” Then they shipped it as Deep Agents: a few thousand lines of core Python code on top of LangGraph. 25,000+ GitHub stars as of June 2026.
In a survey paper submitted in April 2026, Hu Wei and colleagues read 70 agent codebases to see how developers were actually solving production problems, identifying five recurring design dimensions looking at subagent architecture, context management, tool systems…
